message:
build(profile): add release-perf profile for perf-sweep runs

Perf-sweep work needs release-level optimization AND symbolized
frames in the output. The existing \`release\` profile strips
symbols (\`strip = true\`) so flamegraphs from \`samply\` / \`perf\`
land on anonymous addresses and are useless for mapping hot frames
back to source. Adding \`debug = true\` to \`release\` itself would
bloat every distributed binary.

Introduce a dedicated \`release-perf\` profile that:

- \`inherits = \"release\"\` — starts from the same codegen-units,
  \`overflow-checks = false\`, etc. Any future hardening added
  to \`release\` carries across automatically.
- \`opt-level = 3\` — pinned explicitly so the profile doesn't
  silently inherit a future downgrade if \`release\` ever moves.
- \`lto = \"thin\"\` — matches the lto policy we actually want to
  measure; running perf against a non-LTO binary would
  systematically misreport inline-candidate hotspots.
- \`debug = \"line-tables-only\"\` — just enough DWARF for
  \`samply\` / \`perf\` to map samples back to source lines
  without shipping full debug info or bloating the binary with
  type info.
- \`strip = false\` — symbol table must be present for the above
  mapping to work.

Callers opt in with \`cargo build --profile release-perf\` or
\`cargo run --profile release-perf\`; nothing in the standard
\`cargo build --release\` path is affected, so distributed builds
stay stripped.

Pure build-config addition — no runtime code changes.

files.patch:
@@ -39,6 +39,13 @@ strip = true
 [profile.release.package.ftui-extras]
 opt-level = 3
 
+[profile.release-perf]
+inherits = "release"
+opt-level = 3
+lto = "thin"
+debug = "line-tables-only"
+strip = false
+
 [profile.bench]
 debug = true
 strip = false
